evidence.note: Dedicated invariant spec is the stated source of truth and requires stable IDs, prose, machine-checkable predicates, source citations, and status.
Fifteen INV-* entries are listed with prose, predicate or predicate sketch, cite, and status.
Pass-status table maps IDs to STATED/TESTED/PROVED status, but it is not a full spec-coverage matrix mapping every ID to a checking tool.
The only Foundry invariant file contains two invariant_* functions: WETH conservation and a weaker contract-balance-vs-withdrawable check.

The spec marks many invariants as STATED or partial, with only INV-ARB-003 tied to the invariant test file and no comprehensive invariant suite for InstanceSale, FAOSale, FAOTwapResolver, or FutarchyTWAPOracle.
buy is externally state-changing and has implementation guards/effects but no explicit pre/post/frame/revert @spec block.
placeYesBond has useful @dev notes and an INV-ARB-005 tag, but not a pre/post/frame/revert-condition spec block.
buy has @dev bullets that imply preconditions, which matches the low anchor, but this pattern is not complete pre/post coverage.
A Foundry invariant enforces WETH totalSupply conservation across actors plus the arbitration contract.
The ragequit pro-rata law is stated with floor rounding and source citation, but it is not yet a dedicated invariant test.
Unit tests cover multi-token pro-rata rounding, but D4's 7 anchor requires dedicated invariant tests for conservation, pro-rata, and monotonicity.
The design doc states adversary capabilities, non-assumptions, concrete attack vectors A1-A8, and mitigations.
Adversary roles A1, A2, A4, A5, and A6 are described with actions and expected cost/impact bounds.
The wiki threat model is still a stub and explicitly TODO, so the repo does not reach the 7 anchor's defense-to-spec-ID mapping.
The spec defines required spec-to-implementation traceability, but says a future worker pass still needs to attach tags broadly.
A source-level @custom:spec tag references INV-TOKEN-001.
effectiveSupply is tagged to INV-SALE-001 with source behavior matching the cited invariant.
bindProposal and resolve carry @custom:spec tags for INV-TWAP-001 and INV-TWAP-002.
CI installs Foundry and runs forge fmt, forge build, and forge test.
Foundry is configured for the Solidity project, but there is no SMTChecker/model-checker profile here.
No invariant is marked PROVED; the PROVED column is explicitly a future goal once Certora/Halmos is wired.
tx.origin usage is isolated and documented with rationale and security constraints.
A unit test demonstrates migrate pulls staged tokens using tx.origin while msg.sender is the orchestrator.
ragequit loops over ragequitTokens without a stated MAX_RAGEQUIT_TOKENS or upper-bound invariant, blocking the 5 anchor.
The older FAOSale ragequit path has the same dynamic ragequitTokens loop without a formal upper bound.
Assembly exists in math code with source/origin comments, but there is no dedicated semantic model or decidability document.

evaluator_notes: No prior audit/evaluations/topic-3-evals.jsonl entry existed, so per-dimension deltas are null and regressions/improvements are empty. Weakest score is tied across D2, D3, and D7; D2 is listed as the weakest_dimension. I treated vendored lib/openzeppelin-contracts/certora as out of scope for root FAO formal-tool readiness and did not run forge test to avoid mutating build artifacts outside audit/evaluations.

evaluator_notes: Scored requested commit e253784fbea01bf369abbdcafca3b3584655254d against audit/rubrics/topic-3-spec-formalization.md; rubric blob at this commit is 1e46e23a6a398c7133bb27a4b119eb5ef3a1d1f1. The immediately prior Topic 3 JSONL entry in this file was 1b12ea2243da2041d621cb031b61b08184ce16fa, so regression/improvement detection follows the runbook against that actual prior line. The user noted R6 and the e253 CSS token sweep: git diff 2e444c5491df9fc74f9c34e68dcd0761bc550b63..e253784fbea01bf369abbdcafca3b3584655254d touches only site-testnet/styles.css and site-testnet/tokens.css, which is outside Topic 3; no Topic 3 regression is attributable to that CSS-only diff. No score-lowering caps are defined by the Topic 3 rubric. I did not run forge test, Halmos, or SMTChecker because this evaluator is read-only outside audit/evaluations and those tools may write build/log artifacts.

evidence.note: Dedicated invariant spec defines stable INV-* IDs, prose, machine-checkable predicates, citations, status, and proof back-reference rules.
Fifteen top-level invariants are listed with prose, predicates or predicate sketches, source citations, and status.
Representative contract code back-references INV-SALE-001 through INV-SALE-004 with @custom:spec tags.
Per-INV engine assignment matrix maps every INV-* ID to an engine, status, and implementation or planned proof surface; the new token and arbitration symbolic checks are wired into the matrix.
InstanceSale has invariant_* predicates for effectiveSupply, initialPhaseFinalized monotonicity, and token supply accounting.
A second InstanceSale invariant harness adds exact pro-rata payout and non-increasing ETH-per-effective-token ratio predicates.
New Halmos-shaped check_INV_TOKEN_001 obligation covers totalSupply changes across mint, transfer, and burn.
New Halmos-shaped check_INV_ARB_001 obligation covers nextProposalId monotonicity and proposal existence for auto and explicit ids.

FutarchyArbitration still contributes WETH conservation and weaker balance-vs-withdrawable invariant tests; limiting observation: invariant tests still do not cover three core contracts with conservation, monotonicity, and access-control rules each.
InstanceSale mutating functions list PRE, POST, FRAME, REVERTS, events, and touched invariants.
Resolver state-mutating functions have structured PRE/POST/FRAME/REVERTS coverage.
Orchestrator setAdapter and createOfficialProposalAndMigrate have structured pre/post/frame/revert specifications.
Arbitration docs cover several bond, settlement, graduation, and withdraw functions.
Lower-anchor observation: createProposal and createProposalWithId are external state-changing functions not covered in the arbitration precondition file.
Lower-anchor observation: startNextEvaluation, resolveActiveEvaluation, and setEvaluator are external state-changing functions missing from the precondition file, preventing the 7 anchor.
Foundry invariant enforces WETH totalSupply conservation across the closed actor set plus the arbitration contract.
Dedicated invariant tests enforce exact floor-rounded ragequit pro-rata payout and non-increasing post-ragequit treasury ratio.
Dedicated monotonicity invariant asserts initialPhaseFinalized cannot regress once observed.
New Halmos-shaped monotonicity rule proves nextProposalId increases for auto-created proposals and does not regress across explicit-id creation.
Ragequit pro-rata law is stated as a predicate with floor rounding direction and source citation.
Limiting observation: symbolic coverage still does not prove the ragequit pro-rata law, so the full 9 anchor is not met.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: check_INV_ARB_001_idMonotone adds the symbolic nextProposalId monotonicity rule required by one half of the D4 9 anchor.

evidence.note: Threat model defines granted and non-granted attacker capabilities.
Attack vectors are mapped to mechanisms, mitigations, invariant IDs where applicable, and residual risks.
Threats map back to Foundry tests or TODO rows; TODO and bonus-invariant rows prevent the negative-invariant/symbolic-attacker 9 anchor.
Every top-15 invariant includes a source citation.
New token symbolic file carries @custom:spec INV-TOKEN-001 tags and links the proof obligation back to the invariant spec.
New arbitration symbolic file carries @custom:spec INV-ARB-001 tags and links the proof obligation back to the invariant spec.
Engine matrix maps INV-TOKEN-001 and INV-ARB-001 to the new symbolic files while preserving the rest of the INV mapping.
Limiting observation: traceability still describes manual rules/future sweep; no CI script verifies citations resolve, so the 7 anchor is not met.
CI, SMTChecker, and Halmos-oriented profiles are checked in; SMTChecker targets InstanceSale and FAOTwapResolver with CHC.
CI configures SMTChecker and Halmos, runs check_INV_ proof obligations with --loop 3, and uploads halmos.log.
New forge workflow gates build, fmt, unit/integration tests, and invariant_INV_ tests on PR/push.
New token check_INV proof obligation increases the Halmos surface covered by symbolic.yml.
New arbitration check_INV proof obligation increases the Halmos surface covered by symbolic.yml.
Limiting observation: dynamic ragequit-token loop remains without a stated constant bound, blocking the 9 anchor.

scores.anchor_matched: 5 anchor, capped below 7 by unbounded-loop observations

evidence.note: Dedicated decidability document keeps the decision-engine matrix for forge fuzz, Halmos, SMTChecker, and Certora.
Per-INV matrix now points INV-TOKEN-001 to GenericFutarchyToken.symbolic.t.sol and INV-ARB-001 to FutarchyArbitration.symbolic.t.sol.
New bounded symbolic check covers mint, transfer, and burn supply accounting for INV-TOKEN-001.
New bounded symbolic check covers nextProposalId monotonicity and explicit-id creation for INV-ARB-001.
Bounded surfaces and known unbounded surfaces are explicitly enumerated; the ragequit-token loop still needs manual fixed bounds and _queuedCount is only bounded for symbolic analysis.
Lower-anchor observation: ragequit still iterates over ragequitTokens.length without a code-level MAX_RAGEQUIT_TOKENS bound.
Lower-anchor observation: legacy FAOSale has the same dynamic ragequitTokens loop without a formal constant bound.

scores.cap_applied: Capped at 5 because the new symbolic obligations expand the decided set but do not bound every dynamic loop or complete the 7/9 decidability requirements.

improvements.cause: check_INV_ARB_001_idMonotone adds a Halmos-shaped nextProposalId monotonicity rule; symbolic pro-rata is still missing, so this reaches the midpoint below 9.

evaluator_notes: Scored requested commit 535ec42a246382bc3a5b9781c8bf4188425a5799 against audit/rubrics/topic-3-spec-formalization.md; rubric blob is 1e46e23a6a398c7133bb27a4b119eb5ef3a1d1f1. Prior comparison uses the immediately preceding Topic 3 JSONL entry for e253784fbea01bf369abbdcafca3b3584655254d. New Topic 3-relevant lifts are GenericFutarchyToken.symbolic.t.sol, FutarchyArbitration.symbolic.t.sol, and the updated DECIDABILITY.md mapping. The new forge.yml, audit/dashboard/, and CAO worker profiles are primarily Topic 4/5/6 evidence and did not move Topic 3 except as supporting CI/operator context. No regressions were detected. I did not run forge test, Halmos, or SMTChecker because this evaluator is read-only outside audit/evaluations and those tools may write build/log artifacts.

evidence.note: Dedicated invariant spec defines stable INV-* IDs, prose, machine-checkable predicates, citations, status, and proof back-reference rules.
Fifteen top-level invariants are listed with prose, predicates or predicate sketches, source citations, and status.
Representative contract code back-references INV-SALE-001 through INV-SALE-004 with @custom:spec tags.
Per-INV engine matrix maps INV-* IDs to verifier/fuzz engines and now includes concrete symbolic surfaces for token, arbitration, orchestrator, and TWAP checks.
InstanceSale has invariant_* predicates for effectiveSupply, initialPhaseFinalized monotonicity, and token supply accounting.
A second InstanceSale invariant harness adds exact pro-rata payout and non-increasing ETH-per-effective-token ratio predicates.
FutarchyArbitration now has stateful invariant predicates for nextProposalId monotonicity, settlement irreversibility, WETH conservation, and balance >= withdrawable.
New check_INV_TWAP_001 symbolic obligation adds resolver anchor/window determinism coverage, but it is not a stateful invariant suite.

New check_INV_ORCH_002 symbolic obligation covers pre-initialized pool refusal, but D2 still lacks invariant tests for three core contracts with conservation, monotonicity, and access-control rules each.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: FutarchyArbitration gained two stateful invariant_INV_* predicates, moving coverage beyond a single comprehensively covered core contract while still short of the 7 anchor.

evidence.note: InstanceSale mutating functions list PRE, POST, FRAME, REVERTS, events, and touched invariants.
Arbitration docs cover several bond, settlement, graduation, and withdraw functions with PRE/POST/FRAME/REVERTS.
Resolver state-mutating functions have structured PRE/POST/FRAME/REVERTS coverage.
Lower-anchor observation: createProposal and createProposalWithId are external state-changing functions not covered in the arbitration precondition file.
Lower-anchor observation: startNextEvaluation, resolveActiveEvaluation, and setEvaluator are external state-changing functions missing from the precondition file, preventing the 7 anchor.
Foundry invariant retains closed-system WETH conservation and balance >= withdrawable accounting for arbitration.
Dedicated invariant tests enforce exact floor-rounded ragequit pro-rata payout and non-increasing post-ragequit treasury ratio.
Dedicated monotonicity invariant asserts initialPhaseFinalized cannot regress once observed.
Symbolic checks cover nextProposalId monotonicity and settlement irreversibility.
Symbolic check covers resolver anchor monotonicity/window fixity and single resolution.
Limiting observation: symbolic coverage still does not prove the ragequit pro-rata law, so the full 9 anchor is not met.
Threat model defines granted and non-granted attacker capabilities.
Attack vectors are mapped to mechanisms, mitigations, invariant IDs where applicable, and residual risks; A1 maps to INV-ORCH-002.
New symbolic negative check exercises the pre-initialized pool attacker model by asserting an initialized hostile pool is refused and remains unchanged.
Limiting observation: several threat rows still map to TODO tests or bonus invariants, so not every adversary capability is encoded as a testable negative invariant.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: INV-ORCH-002 now has a symbolic negative test for the pool pre-creation attacker model, satisfying part but not all of the 9 anchor.

evaluator_notes: Scored requested commit 030d258e6d7909b3e724f1a7cc5cd7f4f711178c against audit/rubrics/topic-3-spec-formalization.md; rubric blob is 1e46e23a6a398c7133bb27a4b119eb5ef3a1d1f1. Topic 3-relevant lifts are the new FutarchyArbitration stateful invariants, expanded check_INV_* symbolic surface, and updated DECIDABILITY.md mapping. The site-ops dashboard, deployment validation, Etherscan check, fork-mode scripts, and ops dashboard are primarily Topic 4/5/6 evidence and were not allowed to inflate Topic 3 except where they directly support verifier readiness.
